summary: Good soundboard capture of the May‑12‑1991 show, highlighted by a smoking Picasso Moon, soaring Althea and an epic Help > Slip > Franklin’s jam.
review: The recording is a decent sound‑board source – clearer than the alternate tape but still not pristine – and it does justice to a vibrant early‑summer ’91 performance. Fans rave about the powerhouse first set, the fiery Picasso Moon, a soaring Althea, a marathon Help > Slip > Franklin’s, and an unforgettable Deal jam, while Jerry’s vocals are noted as especially strong. Overall the show is regarded as one of the stronger post‑Brent era dates despite some mixed feelings about keyboard textures.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (standout songs, strong vocals) and give the recording at least a 4‑star rating; only a few low‑star or off‑topic comments pull the average down slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several reviewers say this is the better of two sources and note “nice sound” and a solid 4‑star sound quality, though others mention it’s “still not great sounding” and has some muddiness compared to ideal mixes.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as mellow but with great song selection; the second set lifts off with high‑energy jams, especially the long H>S>F sequence and a psychedelic Deal jam that many cite as a show‑high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strong vocals highlighted; his singing is called “strong” and “powerful” throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick’s keyboard work is debated – some criticize cheesy synth samples while others note he eventually found his tone by ’93. Overall keyboards are seen as a mixed element of the era.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum section is praised for lifting the whole show; Bill Kreutzmann’s playing gets indirect nods for driving the jams.
